vulnerabilities.name: command-injection-os-system
vulnerabilities.message: Request data detected in os.system. This could be vulnerable to a command injection and should be avoided. If this must be done, use the 'subprocess' module instead and pass the arguments as a list. See https://owasp.org/www-community/attacks/Command_Injection for more information.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

vulnerabilities.name: os-system-injection
vulnerabilities.message: User data detected in os.system. This could be vulnerable to a command injection and should be avoided. If this must be done, use the 'subprocess' module instead and pass the arguments as a list.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

vulnerabilities.name: dangerous-system-call
vulnerabilities.message: Found user-controlled data used in a system call. This could allow a malicious actor to execute commands. Use the 'subprocess' module instead, which is easier to use without accidentally exposing a command injection vulnerability.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

vulnerabilities.name: subprocess-injection
vulnerabilities.message: Detected user input entering a `subprocess` call unsafely. This could result in a command injection vulnerability. An attacker could use this vulnerability to execute arbitrary commands on the host, which allows them to download malware, scan sensitive data, or run any command they wish on the server. Do not let users choose the command to run. In general, prefer to use Python API versions of system commands. If you must use subprocess, use a dictionary to allowlist a set of commands.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

vulnerabilities.name: dangerous-subprocess-use
vulnerabilities.message: Detected subprocess function 'call' with user controlled data. A malicious actor could leverage this to perform command injection. You may consider using 'shlex.escape()'.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

vulnerabilities.name: subprocess-shell-true
vulnerabilities.message: Found 'subprocess' function 'call' with 'shell=True'. This is dangerous because this call will spawn the command using a shell process. Doing so propagates current shell settings and variables, which makes it much easier for a malicious actor to execute commands. Use 'shell=False' instead.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

vulnerabilities.name: subprocess-injection
vulnerabilities.message: Detected user input entering a `subprocess` call unsafely. This could result in a command injection vulnerability. An attacker could use this vulnerability to execute arbitrary commands on the host, which allows them to download malware, scan sensitive data, or run any command they wish on the server. Do not let users choose the command to run. In general, prefer to use Python API versions of system commands. If you must use subprocess, use a dictionary to allowlist a set of commands.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

vulnerabilities.name: dangerous-subprocess-use
vulnerabilities.message: Detected subprocess function 'Popen' with user controlled data. A malicious actor could leverage this to perform command injection. You may consider using 'shlex.escape()'.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

vulnerabilities.name: subprocess-shell-true
vulnerabilities.message: Found 'subprocess' function 'Popen' with 'shell=True'. This is dangerous because this call will spawn the command using a shell process. Doing so propagates current shell settings and variables, which makes it much easier for a malicious actor to execute commands. Use 'shell=False' instead.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

vulnerabilities.name: avoid-pickle
vulnerabilities.message: Avoid using `pickle`, which is known to lead to code execution vulnerabilities. When unpickling, the serialized data could be manipulated to run arbitrary code. Instead, consider serializing the relevant data as JSON or a similar text-based serialization format.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-502: Deserialization of Untrusted Data

vulnerabilities.name: path-traversal-open
vulnerabilities.message: Found request data in a call to 'open'. Ensure the request data is validated or sanitized, otherwise it could result in path traversal attacks and therefore sensitive data being leaked. To mitigate, consider using os.path.abspath or os.path.realpath or the pathlib library.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

vulnerabilities.name: path-traversal-open
vulnerabilities.message: Found request data in a call to 'open'. Ensure the request data is validated or sanitized, otherwise it could result in path traversal attacks.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

vulnerabilities.name: path-traversal-open
vulnerabilities.message: Found request data in a call to 'open'. Ensure the request data is validated or sanitized, otherwise it could result in path traversal attacks and therefore sensitive data being leaked. To mitigate, consider using os.path.abspath or os.path.realpath or the pathlib library.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

vulnerabilities.name: sql-injection-db-cursor-execute
vulnerabilities.message: User-controlled data from a request is passed to 'execute()'. This could lead to a SQL injection and therefore protected information could be leaked. Instead, use django's QuerySets, which are built with query parameterization and therefore not vulnerable to sql injection. For example, you could use `Entry.objects.filter(date=2006)`.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

vulnerabilities.name: tainted-sql-string
vulnerabilities.message: Detected user input used to manually construct a SQL string. This is usually bad practice because manual construction could accidentally result in a SQL injection. An attacker could use a SQL injection to steal or modify contents of the database. Instead, use a parameterized query which is available by default in most database engines. Alternatively, consider using the Django object-relational mappers (ORM) instead of raw SQL queries.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-915: Improperly Controlled Modification of Dynamically-Determined Object Attributes
vulnerabilities.owasp: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures

vulnerabilities.name: tainted-sql-string
vulnerabilities.message: Detected user input used to manually construct a SQL string. This is usually bad practice because manual construction could accidentally result in a SQL injection. An attacker could use a SQL injection to steal or modify contents of the database. Instead, use a parameterized query which is available by default in most database engines. Alternatively, consider using an object-relational mapper (ORM) such as SQLAlchemy which will protect your queries.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-704: Incorrect Type Conversion or Cast

vulnerabilities.name: formatted-sql-query
vulnerabilities.message: Detected possible formatted SQL query. Use parameterized queries instead.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

vulnerabilities.name: sqlalchemy-execute-raw-query
vulnerabilities.message: Avoiding SQL string concatenation: untrusted input concatenated with raw SQL query can result in SQL Injection. In order to execute raw query safely, prepared statement should be used. SQLAlchemy provides TextualSQL to easily used prepared statement with named parameters. For complex SQL composition, use SQL Expression Language or Schema Definition Language. In most cases, SQLAlchemy ORM will be a better option.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

vulnerabilities.name: sql-injection-db-cursor-execute
vulnerabilities.message: User-controlled data from a request is passed to 'execute()'. This could lead to a SQL injection and therefore protected information could be leaked. Instead, use django's QuerySets, which are built with query parameterization and therefore not vulnerable to sql injection. For example, you could use `Entry.objects.filter(date=2006)`.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

vulnerabilities.name: sqlalchemy-execute-raw-query
vulnerabilities.message: Avoiding SQL string concatenation: untrusted input concatenated with raw SQL query can result in SQL Injection. In order to execute raw query safely, prepared statement should be used. SQLAlchemy provides TextualSQL to easily used prepared statement with named parameters. For complex SQL composition, use SQL Expression Language or Schema Definition Language. In most cases, SQLAlchemy ORM will be a better option.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

vulnerabilities.name: tainted-sql-string
vulnerabilities.message: Detected user input used to manually construct a SQL string. This is usually bad practice because manual construction could accidentally result in a SQL injection. An attacker could use a SQL injection to steal or modify contents of the database. Instead, use a parameterized query which is available by default in most database engines. Alternatively, consider using the Django object-relational mappers (ORM) instead of raw SQL queries.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-915: Improperly Controlled Modification of Dynamically-Determined Object Attributes
vulnerabilities.owasp: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures

vulnerabilities.name: tainted-sql-string
vulnerabilities.message: Detected user input used to manually construct a SQL string. This is usually bad practice because manual construction could accidentally result in a SQL injection. An attacker could use a SQL injection to steal or modify contents of the database. Instead, use a parameterized query which is available by default in most database engines. Alternatively, consider using an object-relational mapper (ORM) such as SQLAlchemy which will protect your queries.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-704: Incorrect Type Conversion or Cast

vulnerabilities.name: ssrf-requests
vulnerabilities.message: Data from request object is passed to a new server-side request. This could lead to a server-side request forgery (SSRF). To mitigate, ensure that schemes and hosts are validated against an allowlist, do not forward the response to the user, and ensure proper authentication and transport-layer security in the proxied request.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-918: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)

vulnerabilities.name: dynamic-urllib-use-detected
vulnerabilities.message: Detected a dynamic value being used with urllib. urllib supports 'file://' schemes, so a dynamic value controlled by a malicious actor may allow them to read arbitrary files. Audit uses of urllib calls to ensure user data cannot control the URLs, or consider using the 'requests' library instead.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-939: Improper Authorization in Handler for Custom URL Scheme

vulnerabilities.name: user-eval
vulnerabilities.message: Found user data in a call to 'eval'. This is extremely dangerous because it can enable an attacker to execute arbitrary remote code on the system. Instead, refactor your code to not use 'eval' and instead use a safe library for the specific functionality you need.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-95: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Dynamically Evaluated Code ('Eval Injection')

vulnerabilities.name: eval-injection
vulnerabilities.message: Detected user data flowing into eval. This is code injection and should be avoided.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-95: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Dynamically Evaluated Code ('Eval Injection')

vulnerabilities.name: eval-detected
vulnerabilities.message: Detected the use of eval(). eval() can be dangerous if used to evaluate dynamic content. If this content can be input from outside the program, this may be a code injection vulnerability. Ensure evaluated content is not definable by external sources.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-95: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Dynamically Evaluated Code ('Eval Injection')

vulnerabilities.name: user-exec
vulnerabilities.message: Found user data in a call to 'exec'. This is extremely dangerous because it can enable an attacker to execute arbitrary remote code on the system. Instead, refactor your code to not use 'eval' and instead use a safe library for the specific functionality you need.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-95: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Dynamically Evaluated Code ('Eval Injection')

vulnerabilities.name: exec-injection
vulnerabilities.message: Detected user data flowing into exec. This is code injection and should be avoided.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-95: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Dynamically Evaluated Code ('Eval Injection')

vulnerabilities.name: exec-detected
vulnerabilities.message: Detected the use of exec(). exec() can be dangerous if used to evaluate dynamic content. If this content can be input from outside the program, this may be a code injection vulnerability. Ensure evaluated content is not definable by external sources.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-95: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Dynamically Evaluated Code ('Eval Injection')

vulnerabilities.name: raw-html-format
vulnerabilities.message: Detected user input flowing into a manually constructed HTML string. You may be accidentally bypassing secure methods of rendering HTML by manually constructing HTML and this could create a cross-site scripting vulnerability, which could let attackers steal sensitive user data. To be sure this is safe, check that the HTML is rendered safely. Otherwise, use templates (`django.shortcuts.render`) which will safely render HTML instead.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

vulnerabilities.name: raw-html-format
vulnerabilities.message: Detected user input flowing into a manually constructed HTML string. You may be accidentally bypassing secure methods of rendering HTML by manually constructing HTML and this could create a cross-site scripting vulnerability, which could let attackers steal sensitive user data. To be sure this is safe, check that the HTML is rendered safely. Otherwise, use templates (`flask.render_template`) which will safely render HTML instead.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

vulnerabilities.name: make-response-with-unknown-content
vulnerabilities.message: Be careful with `flask.make_response()`. If this response is rendered onto a webpage, this could create a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability. `flask.make_response()` will not autoescape HTML. If you are rendering HTML, write your HTML in a template file and use `flask.render_template()` which will take care of escaping. If you are returning data from an API, consider using `flask.jsonify()`.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

vulnerabilities.name: directly-returned-format-string
vulnerabilities.message: Detected Flask route directly returning a formatted string. This is subject to cross-site scripting if user input can reach the string. Consider using the template engine instead and rendering pages with 'render_template()'.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

vulnerabilities.name: raw-html-format
vulnerabilities.message: Detected user input flowing into a manually constructed HTML string. You may be accidentally bypassing secure methods of rendering HTML by manually constructing HTML and this could create a cross-site scripting vulnerability, which could let attackers steal sensitive user data. To be sure this is safe, check that the HTML is rendered safely. Otherwise, use templates (`django.shortcuts.render`) which will safely render HTML instead.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

To be sure this is safe, check that the HTML is rendered safely. Otherwise, use templates (`flask.render_template`) which will safely render HTML instead.
